content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6-max

## Summary

All three amendments identify real, verified defects in the milestone CLI code. Amendment fr-1-fdf3369e covers a cluster of related bead-ID resolution bugs with concrete impact on resume and run paths. Amendment fr-1-ee39a8bd identifies a genuine inconsistency in active-milestone tracking, though the remediation for the status command deserves care since read-only queries shouldn't silently mutate state. Amendment fr-1-168c812b is a straightforward, confirmed UX bug with a clean fix.

## Votes

- `fr-1-fdf3369e`: Accept
  All five references verified against source. (1) `load_bead_detail_from_br` at line 968 uses `details.pop()` which takes the last element arbitrarily when multiple beads match a short ref â€” this is non-deterministic and can resolve to a bead from the wrong milestone. (2) The `bead_status()` impl at lines 1032-1038 propagates `load_bead_detail_from_br` errors as I/O failures instead of mapping them to `ControllerBeadStatus::Missing`, even though the controller at line 933-937 explicitly handles `Missing` as a recoverable state. (3) `project.rs:910` does a strict `bead.id != bead_id` equality check, so short refs stored in the controller that expand to qualified IDs will fail. These interact to create real failure modes: a deleted bead crashes the resume loop instead of being treated as externally closed, and a short ref can resolve to wrong beads or fail the exact-match check.
- `fr-1-ee39a8bd`: Accept
  Verified all four references. `handle_plan` (line 229) validates the explicit milestone ID but the `AwaitingAnswers` early return at line 247 exits before `set_active_milestone` at line 326. `handle_status` with an explicit ID (lines 417-432) returns without updating the active tracking. Meanwhile `resolve_active_milestone` (line 487) reads entirely from the file. This means a user who runs `milestone plan M2` (gets AwaitingAnswers) or `milestone status M2` then runs `milestone next` without an ID will target the previously-active milestone, not M2. The inconsistency is real â€” `handle_next` (line 340) and `handle_show` (line 404) DO update active milestone for explicit IDs. The remediation for `plan` early returns is straightforward; for `status`, the caller is explicitly naming a milestone and similar commands already set it, so updating it is consistent with the existing pattern.
- `fr-1-168c812b`: Accept
  Confirmed: `load_bead_view` at line 920 hardcodes `action: "next"` in the `MilestoneOperationFailed` error. It's called from `inspect_next_milestone_action` (line 543, correct) and `execute_milestone_run` (line 672, incorrect). When the `bead_belongs_to_milestone` check fails during a `milestone run` invocation, the error message will incorrectly say 'milestone next failed'. The fix â€” adding an `action` parameter â€” is clean and low-risk.
